consistency.casting_time_duration_logic: Consistent: A complex, high-level spell with a long casting time and instantaneous effect upon completion.

quality.completeness: High. Contains all standard D&D 5e spell fields (slug, name, level, school, ritual, casting_time, range, components, duration, description, classes).
quality.clarity: Excellent. The effects, especially the revival conditions and the severe after-effects (-4 penalty that fades on long rests), are clearly detailed.
quality.balance_consideration: Appropriate for a 7th-level spell. The cost (1,000 gp diamond consumption) and the significant ongoing mechanical penalty balance the powerful effect of returning a creature to life.

mechanics.revival_conditions: Specific: Must be dead less than a century, not from old age, soul must be free and willing. This prevents reanimation of unwilling undead or extremely ancient/fading souls.
positive_effects: Returns target to life with full HP.
Closes mortal wounds and restores missing body parts.
negative_effects: Target receives a permanent -4 penalty to all rolls, which fades by 1 after each long rest.
If cast on someone dead for a year or more, the caster suffers Disadvantage on all rolls and cannot cast spells until completing a long rest.
mechanics.interaction_with_other_effects: Explicitly states that magical diseases/curses are NOT removed and persist after resurrection, requiring separate removal.

llm_audit.data_archivist_notes: The spell data is complete and mechanically sound for a high-tier restoration effect. The dual penalty system (one for the resurrected, one for the caster in extreme cases) provides good mechanical friction.
